======================== 92 passed, 4 warnings in 0.21s ========================

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Three event types dispatched correctly to `FleetEventSink` methods
criteria_verification.evidence: FleetWatcher.on_fleet_change routes manifest!=None to sink.upsert_agent and manifest=None to sink.remove_agent. FleetWatcher.on_heartbeat validates the AgentHeartbeatPayload and routes to sink.update_heartbeat(agent_id, hb, status_changed). Verified by TestThreeEventTypesDispatched (4 tests, including a direct AsyncMock(spec=FleetEventSink) assertion that each named method is awaited with the correct arguments).
criteria_verification.notes: FleetWatcher.on_fleet_change routes manifest!=None to sink.upsert_agent and manifest=None to sink.remove_agent. FleetWatcher.on_heartbeat validates the AgentHeartbeatPayload and routes to sink.update_heartbeat(agent_id, hb, status_changed). Verified by TestThreeEventTypesDispatched (4 tests, including a direct AsyncMock(spec=FleetEventSink) assertion that each named method is awaited with the correct arguments).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Events failing `AgentManifest.model_validate` / `AgentHeartbeatPayload.model_validate` are logged (WARN) and dropped; subsequent valid events still processed
criteria_verification.evidence: on_heartbeat catches ValidationError/ValueError/TypeError around AgentHeartbeatPayload.model_validate, logs at WARN with the envelope source, and returns without invoking the sink. on_fleet_change catches all non-CancelledError exceptions per dispatch and logs them. Verified by TestMalformedEventsAreLoggedAndDropped (3 tests including a 'bad-then-good' sequence that confirms a malformed heartbeat does not poison subsequent valid heartbeats).
criteria_verification.notes: on_heartbeat catches ValidationError/ValueError/TypeError around AgentHeartbeatPayload.model_validate, logs at WARN with the envelope source, and returns without invoking the sink. on_fleet_change catches all non-CancelledError exceptions per dispatch and logs them. Verified by TestMalformedEventsAreLoggedAndDropped (3 tests including a 'bad-then-good' sequence that confirms a malformed heartbeat does not poison subsequent valid heartbeats).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `status_changed` flag computed correctly (compares previous heartbeat status to new one)
criteria_verification.evidence: FleetWatcher._previous_status reads the cache snapshot via the injected SnapshotReader and falls back to an in-memory mirror. status_changed is computed as `prev_status != hb.status` and forwarded as the third positional argument to sink.update_heartbeat. Verified by TestStatusChangedFlag (3 tests covering first-heartbeat-after-register, repeated same-status, and the cache-view path that observes a stale_sweeper-induced flip).
criteria_verification.notes: FleetWatcher._previous_status reads the cache snapshot via the injected SnapshotReader and falls back to an in-memory mirror. status_changed is computed as `prev_status != hb.status` and forwarded as the third positional argument to sink.update_heartbeat. Verified by TestStatusChangedFlag (3 tests covering first-heartbeat-after-register, repeated same-status, and the cache-view path that observes a stale_sweeper-induced flip).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Re-registration with newer manifest version supersedes (idempotency — no duplicate cache entries)
criteria_verification.evidence: on_fleet_change calls sink.upsert_agent unconditionally for non-None manifests. DiscoveryCache.upsert_agent replaces the entry by agent_id, so a v0.1.0 → v0.2.0 re-register leaves exactly one entry with the newer version. Verified by TestReRegistrationIdempotency.test_reregister_replaces_manifest_no_duplicates (asserts len(snapshot)==1 and version==0.2.0 after two registers for the same agent_id).
criteria_verification.notes: on_fleet_change calls sink.upsert_agent unconditionally for non-None manifests. DiscoveryCache.upsert_agent replaces the entry by agent_id, so a v0.1.0 → v0.2.0 re-register leaves exactly one entry with the newer version. Verified by TestReRegistrationIdempotency.test_reregister_replaces_manifest_no_duplicates (asserts len(snapshot)==1 and version==0.2.0 after two registers for the same agent_id).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `stale_sweeper` marks degraded agents whose `last_heartbeat_at` age exceeds `stale_heartbeat_seconds` (uses injected Clock)
criteria_verification.evidence: run_one_sweep iterates the snapshot, computes age = clock.now() - entry.last_heartbeat_at, and synthesises an AgentHeartbeatPayload(status='degraded') passed to sink.update_heartbeat with status_changed=True when age > threshold. Already-degraded agents are skipped (idempotency). stale_sweeper wraps run_one_sweep in a cancellable loop. Verified by TestStaleSweeper (5 tests: marks-stale, skips-fresh, skips-already-degraded, loop-cancellable, uses-injected-clock).
criteria_verification.notes: run_one_sweep iterates the snapshot, computes age = clock.now() - entry.last_heartbeat_at, and synthesises an AgentHeartbeatPayload(status='degraded') passed to sink.update_heartbeat with status_changed=True when age > threshold. Already-degraded agents are skipped (idempotency). stale_sweeper wraps run_one_sweep in a cancellable loop. Verified by TestStaleSweeper (5 tests: marks-stale, skips-fresh, skips-already-degraded, loop-cancellable, uses-injected-clock).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Racing register+deregister test (asyncio.gather) — final cache state consistent with one-event-wins semantics
criteria_verification.evidence: FleetWatcher delegates all mutation to FleetEventSink (DiscoveryCache), which serialises every write through a single asyncio.Lock — exactly the architectural invariant the AC requires. TestRacingRegisterDeregister.test_concurrent_register_and_deregister_are_consistent fires both events via asyncio.gather and asserts the final snapshot is either {} or {agent_id: entry} but never a torn read.
criteria_verification.notes: FleetWatcher delegates all mutation to FleetEventSink (DiscoveryCache), which serialises every write through a single asyncio.Lock — exactly the architectural invariant the AC requires. TestRacingRegisterDeregister.test_concurrent_register_and_deregister_are_consistent fires both events via asyncio.gather and asserts the final snapshot is either {} or {agent_id: entry} but never a torn read.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `watch` survives transient `nats_client` errors via reconnect loop; test with a `nats_client` mock that raises once then recovers
criteria_verification.evidence: FleetWatcher.run wraps the (subscribe, watch_fleet) calls in `while True` with an asyncio.sleep(reconnect_backoff_seconds) on any non-CancelledError. CancelledError propagates so callers control shutdown. Verified by TestReconnectLoop with a FakeNATSClient that raises RuntimeError on the first watch_fleet call: the test asserts watch_fleet_calls >= 2 and that subscribe was re-invoked, plus a separate test asserting the WARN log was emitted.
criteria_verification.notes: FleetWatcher.run wraps the (subscribe, watch_fleet) calls in `while True` with an asyncio.sleep(reconnect_backoff_seconds) on any non-CancelledError. CancelledError propagates so callers control shutdown. Verified by TestReconnectLoop with a FakeNATSClient that raises RuntimeError on the first watch_fleet call: the test asserts watch_fleet_calls >= 2 and that subscribe was re-invoked, plus a separate test asserting the WARN log was emitted.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
criteria_verification.evidence: ruff check src/forge/adapters/nats/fleet_watcher.py tests/forge/adapters/test_fleet_watcher.py → 'All checks passed!'. mypy src/forge/adapters/nats/fleet_watcher.py → 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file'. Both files use Black-compatible 88-col formatting, snake_case names, full type hints, and Google-style docstrings consistent with the rest of forge.
criteria_verification.notes: ruff check src/forge/adapters/nats/fleet_watcher.py tests/forge/adapters/test_fleet_watcher.py → 'All checks passed!'. mypy src/forge/adapters/nats/fleet_watcher.py → 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file'. Both files use Black-compatible 88-col formatting, snake_case names, full type hints, and Google-style docstrings consistent with the rest of forge.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Independent verification confirmed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 356 chars provided.
